Title: Natural allelic variation of basic helix–loop–helix transcription factor 25 regulates carotenoid biosynthesis in sweet potato

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: basic helix–loop–helix (bHLH) transcription factor, clade IVa; conserved bHLH domain
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Pushu32 (cloned allele); natural promoter haplotypes also examined in Xushu18 and 304 accessions
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Lizixiang (functional validation); storage-root expression also compared across multiple accessions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in storage roots; peaks around 20 days after planting during storage-root thickening/carotenoid biosynthesis; higher in carotenoid-rich orange-fleshed varieties than low-carotenoid white/yellow varieties
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in storage roots of 3-month-old field-grown plants; elevated in carotenoid-rich storage roots
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: β-carotene is a provitamin A precursor; carotenoids act as antioxidants; β-cryptoxanthin has antioxidant, immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory value
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Storage roots; carotenoid globules/chromoplasts in storage-root cells
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes carotenoid accumulation and shifts carotenoid composition toward β-carotene and β-cryptoxanthin in storage roots
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A natural 86-bp promoter insertion allele is associated with higher IbbHLH25 transcription and high-carotenoid orange-fleshed sweet potato, whereas an 86-bp deletion is present in low-carotenoid white/yellow-fleshed varieties. 2. Overexpression of IbbHLH25 in Lizixiang increased total carotenoids by 2.5-fold–6.0-fold, β-carotene by 21.2-fold–55.7-fold, and β-cryptoxanthin by 4.6-fold–9.5-fold, while RNAi caused opposite effects. 3. IbbHLH25 directly binds E-box motifs in IbGGPPS, IbLCYB and IbBCH promoters and activates their transcription. 4. IbbHLH25 forms a heterodimer with IbbHLH66, and co-expression synergistically enhances activation of these carotenoid genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Haplotype/promoter association analysis; promoter-LUC assay; overexpression and RNAi transgenics; carotenoid profiling by UPLC-APCI-MS/MS; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization; transactivation assay; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-LUC; Y2H; BiFC; LCI; co-IP
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates IbGGPPS, IbLCYB and IbBCH transcription by binding promoter E-box motifs; broadly upregulates many carotenoid biosynthetic genes indirectly/secondarily
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: carotenoid biosynthesis; branch output toward β-carotene and β-cryptoxanthin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core regulatory factor and major positive controller of carotenoid accumulation/composition in sweet potato storage roots
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: IbbHLH66; IbMYB73 (identified in Y2H but not supported as metabolically effective in this study)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms IbbHLH25–IbbHLH66 heterodimer that synergistically activates carotenoid biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Natural promoter allelic variation: an 86-bp insertion upstream of the transcription start site increases promoter activity; a linked promoter SNP/haplotype is associated with high carotenoid content
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown plants; storage-root developmental stage, especially around 20 DAP and harvest at 120 DAP, is important for interpretation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used for overexpression/GFP/ChIP constructs; native IbbHLH25 promoters tested in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome re-analysis using public datasets: Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.7184909; NGDC PRJCA008295; DDBJ DRA010350; NCBI SRP068179; resequencing/haplotype data from Xiao et al. (2023)
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: IbbHLH25 overexpression decreased dry matter by 6.9%–13.1% and starch by 4.2%–6.3%, while increasing soluble sugars by 19.1%–34.7%
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for carotenoid biofortification; the 86-bp promoter indel can serve as a molecular marker for early selection of high-carotenoid varieties; promoter editing or expression enhancement is suggested to improve carotenoid content, potentially together with IbbHLH66
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Selected as the core gene after IbNAC86 showed no genetic association with carotenoid content. Binds specific E-boxes (e.g. CACGTG/CATGTG). N-terminal 85 aa are critical for transactivation.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Not detailed in this paper; function assessed in storage roots of transgenic plants
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: β-carotene is a provitamin A precursor; carotenoids and β-cryptoxanthin contribute antioxidant and health-promoting properties
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Storage roots; carotenoid globules/chromoplasts in storage-root cells
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes carotenoid accumulation and increases the proportions of β-carotene and β-cryptoxanthin in storage roots
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IbbHLH66 was identified as an IbbHLH25-interacting bHLH protein and forms heterodimers with IbbHLH25 in vitro and in vivo. 2. Overexpression of IbbHLH66 increased total carotenoids by 2.3-fold–3.8-fold, β-carotene by 15.2-fold–25.6-fold, and β-cryptoxanthin by 3.1-fold–5.1-fold in storage roots. 3. IbbHLH66 directly binds and activates IbGGPPS, IbLCYB and IbBCH, targeting the same sites as IbbHLH25. 4. Co-expression with IbbHLH25 synergistically enhances activation of target promoters and carotenoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H screening/verification; overexpression transgenics; carotenoid profiling by UPLC-APCI-MS/MS; qRT-PCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; dual-LUC; BiFC; LCI; co-IP
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates IbGGPPS, IbLCYB and IbBCH transcription; upregulates nearly all tested carotenoid pathway genes in overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator that works alone and as a synergistic heterodimer partner of IbbHLH25 to promote carotenoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms IbbHLH25–IbbHLH66 heterodimer with synergistic promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used for ChIP and overexpression-related assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unlike IbbHLH25, overexpression increased dry matter (11.2%–16.1%), starch (8.6%–15.4%), and soluble sugar (14.7%–24.5%) alongside carotenoids
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising biofortification regulator; may be especially useful because it raises carotenoids while also improving dry matter/starch/sugars, potentially helping balance nutritional and quality traits; suitable for stacking with IbbHLH25
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Directly targets the same promoter binding sites in IbGGPPS, IbLCYB and IbBCH as IbbHLH25; no RNAi/knockout analysis reported in this paper.
